name: Labrador Retriever
notes: Prone to hip/elbow dysplasia and obesity. Healthy weight management saves significant routine-vet cost over a lifetime.

name: French Bulldog
notes: Brachycephalic — higher rates of respiratory issues, allergies, and C-section births. Insurance is often a strong fit for this breed.

name: Golden Retriever
notes: Coat requires regular grooming. Hip dysplasia common. Generally healthy and responsive to vet care.

name: German Shepherd
notes: Hip dysplasia is common. High energy requires good nutrition and preventive care.

name: Bulldog
notes: Brachycephalic with frequent respiratory, skin, and joint issues. One of the higher-cost breeds.

name: Maine Coon
notes: Long coat means real grooming costs. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) screening is recommended.

name: Persian
notes: Extensive grooming required to prevent matting. Prone to eye issues and respiratory problems.

name: Siamese
notes: Generally healthy. Low grooming needs. Minimal breed-specific health issues.

name: Ragdoll
notes: Large breed with moderate grooming needs. Generally healthy with good lifespan.

name: Chihuahua
notes: Small, generally healthy. Dental disease and patellar luxation are the most common cost drivers. Long lifespan spreads costs across more years.

name: Poodle (Standard)
notes: Hypoallergenic coat needs professional grooming every 4-8 weeks. Generally healthy compared to other large breeds.

name: Dachshund
notes: Long-bodied breed with elevated risk of intervertebral disc disease (IVDD). Surgery for severe cases can run $4,000-$8,000.

name: Rottweiler
notes: Large body, deep chest. Hip/elbow dysplasia, bone cancer risk, and bloat (GDV) are documented. Anesthesia and surgical costs scale with size.

name: Australian Shepherd
notes: Heavy double coat needs regular brushing. Watch for MDR1 gene mutation affecting drug sensitivity — ask the vet to flag.

name: Pit Bull / American Staffordshire Terrier
notes: Generally healthy; skin allergies and ACL tears are most common. Insurance and rental policies sometimes apply breed-specific rules — check before adopting.

name: Beagle
notes: Generally healthy. Watch obesity (food-driven breed) and ear infections (drop ears).

name: Boxer
notes: Cancer-prone breed. Brachycephalic features and cardiac issues (boxer cardiomyopathy) drive lifetime costs up.

name: Yorkshire Terrier
notes: Long silky coat needs grooming. Dental disease and tracheal collapse are the most common cost drivers.

name: Siberian Husky
notes: Heavy double coat (massive shedding). Generally healthy but eye conditions and hip dysplasia documented.

name: Doberman Pinscher
notes: Dilated cardiomyopathy and von Willebrand disease (bleeding disorder) are documented. Pre-purchase health screening matters.

name: Pug
notes: Brachycephalic — BOAS surgery is common. Eye injuries from prominent eyes. Skin fold infections.

name: Shih Tzu
notes: Long coat needs frequent grooming. Eye and dental issues common.

name: Cavalier King Charles Spaniel
notes: Mitral valve disease (heart) and syringomyelia (neurological) are common breed risks. Insurance is often a strong fit.

name: Boston Terrier
notes: Brachycephalic features. Eye injuries and corneal ulcers common.

name: Mastiff
notes: Hip dysplasia and bloat are documented. Short lifespan due to giant size; large food and anesthesia costs.

name: Great Dane
notes: Bloat (GDV) is a major risk — preventive gastropexy is common. Cardiac issues and very short lifespan.

name: Pomeranian
notes: Patellar luxation, dental disease, and tracheal collapse. Long-lifespan breed.

name: Border Collie
notes: Generally healthy. Hip dysplasia and Collie eye anomaly possible. High-energy lifestyle adds daycare/training costs.

name: Bernese Mountain Dog
notes: Cancer-prone breed (especially histiocytic sarcoma). Hip and elbow dysplasia. Very short lifespan.

name: Cane Corso
notes: Hip dysplasia and eyelid conditions (entropion/ectropion). Massive food and anesthesia cost lines.

name: Miniature Schnauzer
notes: Pancreatitis-prone (avoid fatty foods). Regular grooming required to maintain coat.

name: Cocker Spaniel
notes: Recurring ear infections (drop ears + heavy fur). Eye conditions. Regular grooming a must.

name: Australian Cattle Dog
notes: Healthy breed. Very high energy — many owners use daycare or hire walkers. Deafness can occur.

name: Newfoundland
notes: Hip and elbow dysplasia, cardiac issues (subaortic stenosis), drooling. Very large food and anesthesia bills.

name: Bengal
notes: Active and playful. HCM screening recommended. Some states regulate ownership of early-generation Bengals.

name: British Shorthair
notes: Generally healthy. HCM and PKD screening recommended in breeding lines.

name: Sphynx
notes: Hairless cats need weekly bathing, sun protection, and warmth. HCM screening recommended.

name: Scottish Fold
notes: Osteochondrodysplasia (joint disease) is intrinsic to the folded-ear gene. Veterinary associations have raised welfare concerns about the breed.
